howItWorks.intro: This purchasing power calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Future Value = Present Value × (1 + Inflation Rate)^Years
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How much has the dollar lost in purchasing power?
faq.answer: Since 2000, the US dollar has lost roughly 45% of its purchasing power. Since 1913 (when the Federal Reserve was created), it has lost over 96% of its value.

howWeCalculate.intro: This purchasing power calculator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.
steps: Enter your specific values into the calculator fields above
Our algorithm applies the relevant formulas using your inputs

howWeCalculate.disclaimer: These calculations are estimates based on standard formulas. For critical decisions, always consult a qualified professional.
